ddls:
CREATE TABLE "themes" (
  "theme_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each theme */,
  "theme_name" TEXT /* Name of the research theme */,
  "total_studies" INTEGER /* Total number of studies related to the theme */,
  "hci_studies" INTEGER /* Number of HCI-related studies */,
  "non_hci_studies" INTEGER /* Number of non-HCI studies */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the theme and its significance */,
  "theme_category" TEXT /* Category of the theme (e.g., economic models, societal costs) */,
  "sub_theme" TEXT /* Sub-theme or related field of study */,
  "theme_status" TEXT /* Status of the theme (e.g., active, inactive, merged) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("theme_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institutional affiliation of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department within the institution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the study */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the study was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_device" TEXT /* Device used to access the study (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytic entry */,
  "theme_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the theme being analyzed */,
  "total_views" INTEGER /* Total views of studies within the theme */,
  "total_downloads" INTEGER /* Total downloads of studies within the theme */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the analytic data was last updated */,
  "average_access_time" REAL /* Average time spent accessing studies in the theme */,
  "top_contributors" TEXT /* Top contributors to the theme (e.g., authors, researchers) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_theme_id FOREIGN KEY ("theme_id") REFERENCES themes ("theme_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "collaborations" (
  "collab_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each collaboration entry */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study being collaborated on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user involved in the collaboration */,
  "role_in_study" TEXT /* Role of the user in the study (e.g., lead researcher, co-author) */,
  "contribution_type" TEXT /* Type of contribution made by the user (e.g., data collection, writing) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("collab_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_collaborations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study using the data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the data source (e.g., survey, dataset) */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date the data source was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_trails" (
  "trail_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit trail entry */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study related to the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken (e.g., added, updated, deleted) */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date of the action */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who made the change */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Detailed description of the action taken */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trail_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_trails_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "study_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study associated with the tag */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "theme_relationships" (
  "rel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "theme_id1" INTEGER /* ID of the first theme in the relationship */,
  "theme_id2" INTEGER /* ID of the second theme in the relationship */,
  "relationship_type" TEXT /* Type of relationship between the themes (e.g., related, similar) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rel_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_theme_relationships_theme_id1 FOREIGN KEY ("theme_id1") REFERENCES themes ("theme_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_theme_relationships_theme_id2 FOREIGN KEY ("theme_id2") REFERENCES themes ("theme_id")
)
